title: Distinguishing Probability Mass Functions (PMF) and Probability Density Functions (PDF)
description: A flowchart that guides the user through the process of identifying the correct probability function (PMF or PDF) based on the type of random variable (discrete or continuous) and then details how to calculate mathematical expectation for each.
elements: Is the Random Variable Discrete or Continuous?
Calculate Expectation for Discrete Variable: E[X] = Σ x * p(x)
Calculate Expectation for Continuous Variable: E[X] = ∫ x * f(x) dx
relationships: The Start leads to the question 'Discrete or Continuous?'
'Use PMF' leads to the discrete expectation formula
'Use PDF' leads to the continuous expectation formula
Both expectation calculations lead to the final outcome 'End (Expected Value Found)'
